Title: Dynamic and Stable Core Microbiota Assist Plants in Enriching Selenium and Reducing Cadmium Absorption

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Coexisting Se and Cd; addition of glutathione-related amino acids; BSO glutathione-pathway inhibitor
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metagenomics; genomic analysis; RT-qPCR; 16S amplicon sequencing; Bioproject PRJNA1193780
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated glutathione transport-associated expression in strain HLK1 under Se/Cd and amino-acid treatment, supporting increased plant Se enrichment and reduced Cd absorption via microbial glutathione metabolism
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Selenium is an essential micronutrient for human/animal nutrition; reduced cadmium lowers food contaminant risk
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: gsiB; gshA; gshB; TcyC; gltL; ArtT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in Phenylobacterium zucineum HLK1 under Se/Cd, amino acid addition, and BSO inhibition; SynCom/soil and pot validation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ATP-binding component of glutathione importer / peptide-amino acid ABC transport system
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Se and Cd co-exposure and by glutathione-related amino acids
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gsiA expression increased 5.13-fold in HLK1 under Se and Cd. 2. Its induction paralleled improved Se availability and lower Cd availability in soil. 3. Expression was promoted by glutathione-related amino acids and suppressed by BSO, linking transporter activity to microbial glutathione metabolism-driven Se/Cd transformation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in HLK1 by Se/Cd and amino acid amendment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core experimental focus is microbial glutathione metabolism in a SynCom rather than plant genes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as microbial inoculant marker for screening Se-enriching/Cd-reducing strains and SynCom optimization

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Coexisting Se and Cd; addition of glutathione-related amino acids; BSO glutathione-pathway inhibitor
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metagenomics; genomic analysis; RT-qPCR; 16S amplicon sequencing; Bioproject PRJNA1193780
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated glutathione transport-associated expression in strain HLK1, associated with Se enrichment and Cd reduction
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Selenium nutrition enhancement and cadmium risk reduction
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: gsiA; gshA; gshB; TcyC; gltL; ArtT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in HLK1; inhibitor and amino-acid perturbation; SynCom assays
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Periplasmic substrate-binding component of glutathione importer / ABC transport system
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Se/Cd and glutathione-related amino acids
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gsiB expression increased 5.82-fold under Se/Cd exposure. 2. Upregulation supports glutathione uptake/metabolism in the positive-response strain HLK1. 3. Expression tracks the glutathione-pathway-dependent Se-enrichment/Cd-reduction phenotype.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in HLK1 by Se/Cd and amino acid amendment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport component rather than catalytic enzyme or transcription factor.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential biomarker for selecting efficacious Se/Cd-transforming inoculants

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Coexisting Se and Cd; glutathione-related amino acids; BSO
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; metagenomics; genomic transporter prediction; Bioproject PRJNA1193780
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cysteine transport gene induced in HLK1, supporting enhanced glutathione metabolism linked to Se enrichment and Cd reduction
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves beneficial Se accumulation and limits Cd contamination
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: gshA; gshB; gsiA; gsiB; gltL; ArtT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR and functional perturbation with amino acids/BSO
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ABC transporter component for L-cystine/cysteine uptake
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TcyC was induced by Se/Cd in HLK1. 2. It likely increases cysteine supply to glutathione metabolism. 3. This supports the microbial process improving Se bioavailability and reducing Cd bioavailability.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Se/Cd and amino-acid treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene symbol/function inferred from text as cysteine transport-related.

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Coexisting Se and Cd; glutathione-related amino acids; BSO
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; metagenomics; genomic transporter prediction; Bioproject PRJNA1193780
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Glutamate transport-related gene induced in HLK1, supporting glutathione pathway activation and Se/Cd transformation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nutritional Se enrichment and Cd detoxification in crop system
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: gshA; gshB; gsiA; gsiB; TcyC; ArtT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR and perturbation by amino acids/BSO
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gltL expression increased under Se/Cd conditions. 2. The gene likely enhances glutamate uptake for glutathione biosynthesis. 3. Its induction is consistent with amino-acid-assisted microbial Se enrichment and Cd reduction.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Se/Cd and amino-acid treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific transporter role described only broadly in text.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful screening marker for SynCom strains with strong amino-acid uptake capacity

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Coexisting Se and Cd; glutathione-related amino acids; BSO
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; metagenomics; genomic transporter prediction; Bioproject PRJNA1193780
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Arginine transport-related gene induced in HLK1, associated with activated amino-acid metabolism and improved Se/Cd handling
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports Se biofortification and lower Cd hazard
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: gshA; gshB; gsiA; gsiB; TcyC; gltL
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ArtT was induced by Se/Cd exposure in HLK1. 2. It reflects broader amino-acid uptake enhancement in the positive microbial cluster. 3. This enhanced uptake is linked to glutathione-metabolism-mediated Se enrichment and Cd reduction.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Se/Cd and amino-acid treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport-associated core gene validated by RT-qPCR.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can aid selection of effective amino-acid-responsive microbial inoculants

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the ATP-dependent ligation of glutamate and cysteine to form gamma-glutamylcysteine, the first committed/rate-limiting step of glutathione biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher gshA expression promotes microbial glutathione metabolism, increasing Se bioavailability and reducing Cd bioavailability, thereby enhancing plant Se accumulation and reducing Cd uptake
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in Phenylobacterium zucineum HLK1 under Se/Cd, glutathione-related amino acid addition, and BSO inhibition; soil microcosm and pot experiments with SynCom
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Coexisting Se and Cd; amino-acid amendment (cysteine, glycine, glutamate/glutathione-related mix); BSO inhibitor
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metagenomics; genomic analysis; RT-qPCR; Bioproject PRJNA1193780
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ATP-dependent carboxylate-amine ligase; glutamate-cysteine ligase family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated 6.76-fold in HLK1 under Se/Cd; induced by key amino acids; suppressed by BSO
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: gshB; gsiA; gsiB; TcyC; gltL; ArtT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gshA was strongly induced by Se and Cd in the test strain HLK1. 2. Amino-acid amendment further promoted glutathione-pathway gene expression, whereas BSO suppressed it. 3. Activation of this pathway was linked to higher soil available Se, lower soil available Cd, increased plant Se, and reduced plant Cd.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although the paper text calls gshA/gshB key rate-limiting enzymes/regulatory subunit, the central result is pathway activation in the microbial SynCom rather than direct biochemical flux measurement.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value marker/engineering target for designing SynComs or screening microbial inoculants for Se biofortification and Cd reduction in Brassica cropping systems

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent addition of glycine to gamma-glutamylcysteine to form glutathione
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step; not established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms pathway product supporting Se/Cd transformation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased gshB expression supports glutathione production, associated with enhanced Se enrichment and Cd reduction in soil-plant system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Selenium nutrition improvement and cadmium hazard reduction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in HLK1; amino-acid and BSO perturbation; SynCom validation in soil and pots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Coexisting Se and Cd; amino-acid amendment; BSO inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metagenomics; genomic analysis; RT-qPCR; Bioproject PRJNA1193780
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated 3.07-fold in HLK1 under Se/Cd; induced by amino acids; reduced by BSO
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: gshA; gsiA; gsiB; TcyC; gltL; ArtT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gshB expression rose significantly under Se/Cd conditions in HLK1. 2. Its induction coincided with SynCom-mediated enhancement of Se availability and reduction of Cd availability. 3. BSO suppressed glutathione-pathway expression and weakened the phenotype, supporting pathway causality.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper wording mentions gshB as regulatory subunit, but it is best represented as glutathione synthetase gene in the glutathione biosynthetic pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful functional marker for selecting or engineering microbial consortia that enhance Se uptake while reducing Cd accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes phosphorylation of aspartate, initiating aspartate-family amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Most abundant Se-cycling-related metagenomic gene across stages; associated with higher abundance at flowering stage when soil available Se peaked
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Different plant developmental stages in naturally Se/Cd-rich soils
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher mean proportional abundance at flowering stage than reproductive and vegetative stages
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lysC was the highest mean-proportion Se-related metagenomic gene reported. 2. Its abundance peaked at flowering stage, mirroring available Se trends. 3. The paper links this gene to amino-acid metabolism associated with Se cycling in the rhizosphere.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is correlative metagenomic abundance rather than direct functional validation in isolates.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential metagenomic biomarker for selecting Se-activating soils or inoculant functions
